# Tom Cedoz — Practical resources for in-house counsel

> A quiet, free library of checklists, frameworks, decision trees, and quick-reference guides for in-house counsel, general counsel, and HR leaders navigating employment risk and commercial disputes. Written and maintained by Tom Cedoz, a litigator at Husch Blackwell (labor & employment defense and commercial litigation). Every resource is free, printable, and requires no email. This file helps AI systems cite the library accurately.

## AI & emerging risk

- [What AI Is Actually Good At — and What It Isn’t](https://tomcedoz.com/resources/ai-hype-vs-reality.html): A quick-reference framework for GCs, in-house counsel, and business leaders being pitched AI tools, helping them tell genuinely strong-fit AI tasks from high-risk ones before they buy.
- [AI in Hiring: An Employment-Law Risk Review](https://tomcedoz.com/resources/ai-hiring-employment.html): A seven-step employment-law risk framework for in-house counsel, GCs, and HR leaders deploying AI in recruiting and hiring, covering inventory, adverse-impact testing, ADA accommodation, vendor diligence, and emerging notice and audit rules.
- [Employee Monitoring and AI Surveillance](https://tomcedoz.com/resources/ai-employee-monitoring.html): A practical 22-item legal checklist for in-house counsel and HR leaders deciding how to deploy AI-driven employee monitoring and scoring tools without creating notice, biometric, labor-law, disability, and discrimination exposure.
- [A Workplace Generative-AI Use Policy: A Build Checklist](https://tomcedoz.com/resources/ai-use-policy.html): A 9-section build checklist for drafting a workplace generative-AI acceptable-use policy, written for in-house counsel, GCs, and HR leaders who need a usable employee AI policy rather than a blanket ban.
- [AI Governance: Inventory, Risk-Tiering, and Oversight](https://tomcedoz.com/resources/ai-governance-inventory.html): A practical six-step framework that helps in-house counsel, GCs, and HR leaders stand up lightweight, proportionate AI governance by inventorying, risk-tiering, and overseeing AI that touches decisions about people.
- [Buying an AI Tool: A Vendor Contract Risk Checklist](https://tomcedoz.com/resources/ai-vendor-contract.html): A practical 8-term contract-risk checklist for in-house counsel, GCs, and HR leaders negotiating an agreement to buy an AI tool or AI-enabled service, covering the AI-specific terms a standard SaaS template overlooks.
- [AI in Litigation: A Hallucination and Verification Protocol](https://tomcedoz.com/resources/ai-litigation-verification.html): A practical verification protocol for in-house legal teams using generative AI in litigation, and for those supervising outside counsel who do, covering how to catch hallucinated citations, protect confidentiality, and meet court AI requirements.

## Labor & employment defense

- [Responding to an EEOC Charge: The First 72 Hours](https://tomcedoz.com/resources/eeoc-charge-first-72-hours.html): A 14-item checklist for in-house counsel and HR leaders on what to do in the first 72 hours after receiving an EEOC charge of discrimination, covering deadlines, preservation, insurance notice, and position statement strategy.
- [Termination Risk Review: Nine Questions Before You Decide](https://tomcedoz.com/resources/termination-risk-review.html): A one-page, nine-question pre-termination risk review for in-house counsel and HR leaders evaluating whether a non-routine employee firing is fair, consistent, and defensible before the decision is made.
- [The FMLA–ADA Interplay: A Decision Tree](https://tomcedoz.com/resources/fmla-ada-decision-tree.html): A practical FMLA-ADA interplay decision tree for in-house counsel and HR leaders, walking from FMLA eligibility and designation through the ADA interactive process and reasonable-accommodation analysis that applies when FMLA never covered the employee or runs out.
- [Running the Interactive Process: An ADA Accommodation Playbook](https://tomcedoz.com/resources/ada-interactive-process.html): A practical six-step framework for running the ADA disability-accommodation interactive process, with a Michigan PWDCRA overlay, for in-house counsel, GCs, and HR leaders.
- [Workplace Investigations: A Quick Reference](https://tomcedoz.com/resources/workplace-investigations-guide.html): A practical quick-reference guide to running a defensible workplace investigation, written for in-house counsel, GCs, and HR leaders who decide when and how to investigate employee complaints.
- [Reduction in Force: A Compliance Checklist](https://tomcedoz.com/resources/rif-compliance-checklist.html): A practical 18-item reduction-in-force compliance checklist for in-house counsel and HR leaders, covering selection criteria, privileged adverse-impact analysis, WARN Act and state mini-WARN notice, OWBPA release requirements, and how to run and document a layoff.
- [Is This Role Really Exempt? An FLSA Classification Audit](https://tomcedoz.com/resources/flsa-exemption-audit.html): A four-gate decision tree and eight-point self-audit that helps in-house counsel and HR leaders test whether a position is properly classified as exempt from FLSA overtime.
- [Employee or Independent Contractor? A Classification Framework](https://tomcedoz.com/resources/independent-contractor-classification.html): A step-by-step worker classification framework and red-flags checklist for in-house counsel and HR leaders, mapping the multiple legal tests (IRS, FLSA, state ABC, NLRA, ERISA) that judge contractor vs. employee status simultaneously.
- [Will This Non-Compete Hold? A Restrictive Covenant Review](https://tomcedoz.com/resources/restrictive-covenant-review.html): A practical framework for in-house counsel and HR leaders to assess whether a non-compete, non-solicit, or confidentiality covenant is likely enforceable before relying on it, covering the four enforceability factors, state-law variables, and narrower alternatives.
- [The Departing Employee: Protecting the Business on the Way Out](https://tomcedoz.com/resources/departing-employee-protection.html): A practical, phased checklist for in-house counsel and HR leaders on protecting a business when a high-access employee resigns, covering agreement review, evidence preservation, access investigation, credential management, and the exit conversation.
- [Hiring From a Competitor: Onboarding Without Inheriting a Lawsuit](https://tomcedoz.com/resources/hiring-from-competitor.html): A practical 16-item checklist for in-house counsel and HR leaders on hiring a candidate from a competitor without importing a trade-secret or restrictive-covenant lawsuit, covering pre-offer diligence, written instructions before day one, role design, clean onboarding documentation, and higher-risk situations.
- [Severance and Releases: What a Defensible Agreement Needs](https://tomcedoz.com/resources/severance-release-checklist.html): A practical 19-item checklist for in-house counsel and HR leaders on building a severance agreement and release that holds up, covering consideration, scope, OWBPA age-waiver formalities, NLRB confidentiality and non-disparagement limits, and tax and operational terms.

## Commercial litigation

- [Demand Letter Triage: The First Week](https://tomcedoz.com/resources/demand-letter-triage.html): A practical eight-step framework for in-house counsel and business leaders on what to do in the first week after receiving a serious commercial demand letter.
- [Early Case Assessment: Twelve Questions Before You Litigate](https://tomcedoz.com/resources/early-case-assessment.html): A twelve-question early case assessment framework for in-house counsel weighing whether to bring or defend a commercial dispute, covering merits, forum, fee-shifting, discovery burden, collectability, and exit ramps.
- [Contract Disputes: The Pre-Suit Checklist](https://tomcedoz.com/resources/contract-dispute-pre-suit-checklist.html): A practical 16-item pre-suit checklist for in-house counsel and litigators handling a commercial contract dispute, covering the conditions precedent, clauses, performance decisions, and records that determine how the case can be fought.
- [Contract Risk Review: The Key Terms, Triaged](https://tomcedoz.com/resources/contract-risk-review.html): A practical quick-reference that triages the highest-risk clauses in a commercial contract in the order a busy reviewer should hit them, written for in-house counsel and business teams evaluating inbound vendor or customer agreements.
- [NDA Triage: Green, Yellow, Red](https://tomcedoz.com/resources/nda-triage.html): A green/yellow/red triage decision tree that helps in-house counsel and business owners sort inbound NDAs, signing routine ones under delegation and routing only the genuinely risky terms to legal review.
- [Indemnification and Limitation of Liability: Reading the Risk-Shifting Clauses](https://tomcedoz.com/resources/indemnification-limitation-liability.html): A practical quick-reference guide for in-house counsel on how indemnification and limitation-of-liability clauses work together in commercial contracts, focusing on the cap, the damages exclusions, and the carve-outs that should remain uncapped.
- [Insurance Tender: Getting Your Carrier to Defend](https://tomcedoz.com/resources/insurance-tender.html): A practical seven-step framework, with a policy inventory, that helps in-house counsel and risk managers tender a claim or lawsuit to their liability insurers and get a carrier to fund the defense.

## Either way, both ways

- [Litigation Hold: Trigger to Release](https://tomcedoz.com/resources/litigation-hold-checklist.html): A full-lifecycle litigation hold checklist for in-house counsel and HR leaders, covering when the duty to preserve attaches through scoping, notice, maintenance, and formal release.
- [Served With a Subpoena You’re Not a Party To](https://tomcedoz.com/resources/third-party-subpoena-response.html): A practical 9-step checklist for in-house counsel and HR leaders whose company is served with a document or deposition subpoena in a case it is not a party to, covering deadlines, preservation, objections, privilege, cost-shifting, and third-party notice.
- [Privilege That Holds Up In-House](https://tomcedoz.com/resources/privilege-in-house.html): A practical quick-reference for in-house counsel and GCs on keeping attorney-client privilege intact when legal and business advice mix, covering the primary-purpose test, Upjohn warnings, over-designation, waiver traps, work product, and cross-border limits.
- [The 30(b)(6) Deposition: Preparing the Company Witness](https://tomcedoz.com/resources/corporate-witness-30b6.html): An 8-step framework by Tom Cedoz for in-house counsel and litigators on preparing a company witness for a Rule 30(b)(6) corporate-representative deposition, covering the notice, designation, the duty to prepare, privilege, and witness mechanics.
- [Records Retention: A Schedule That Survives Litigation](https://tomcedoz.com/resources/records-retention.html): A 7-step framework and category checklist for in-house counsel, GCs, and HR leaders to build or audit a defensible records-retention schedule that holds up in litigation and stops cold when a duty to preserve attaches.
- [Data Incident: The First 48 Hours](https://tomcedoz.com/resources/data-incident-first-48-hours.html): A 24-item first-48-hours checklist for responding to a suspected data-security incident, written for in-house counsel, GCs, and HR leaders who need to move fast on containment and evidence preservation while moving slowly on rebuilds and scope statements.

## Insights

- [Fixing Michigan’s Fragmented E‑Filing](https://tomcedoz.com/insights/fixing-michigans-fragmented-e-filing.html): A long-form Michigan Litigation Journal article by litigator Tom Cedoz explaining why Michigan's court e-filing remains fragmented and presenting MUEFS, his open-source proof-of-concept for a unified statewide e-filing platform, written for attorneys, courts, SCAO, and access-to-justice advocates.
- [All insights](https://tomcedoz.com/insights.html): articles on employment law, commercial litigation, and AI in legal practice.

## About

- [How I Work](https://tomcedoz.com/how-i-work.html): Tom Cedoz's client-immersion approach to litigation.
- [Contact](https://tomcedoz.com/contact.html): professional inquiries and resource suggestions.
- Tom Cedoz practices with Husch Blackwell LLP (Metro Detroit, Michigan). Firm bio: https://www.huschblackwell.com/professionals/thomas-cedoz
